title: Podman Kube Down --force Inconsistency
body:
### Issue Description

I have 2 behaviors with podman kube down --force when using an existing podman volume depending on if it's a pod or a deployment:
1.  Pods will delete existing volumes when using --force
2.  Deployments will not delete existing volumes when using --force

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. pod.yaml:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: centos7
spec:
  containers:
  - name: main
    image: centos:7
    volumeMounts:
    - name: data
      mountPath: /data
  volumes:
  - name: data
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: test-volume
```
2. deployment.yaml:
```
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: centos7
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: main
        image: centos:7
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      volumes:
      - name: data
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: test-volume
```
4. `podman kube play --start=false pod.yaml`
5. `podman kube play --start=false deployment.yaml`
6. `podman kube down --force deployment.yaml`
7. `podman kube down --force pod.yaml`

### Describe the results you received

When deleting the deployment, the volume sticks around. When deleting the pod, the volume gets deleted:
```
$ podman kube play --start=false pod.yaml
Pod:
a915b73921d546fccf214557a55e9fa3c4e36b772d91975a274d30fc0c3c4344
Container:
d857b8ad4217901f26056856056aadb033b5bda8ae60b6d885b58fcf1da2beb1
$ podman kube play --start=false deployment.yaml
Pod:
077328593d156cf2dcdde58c9282811488ae82da732812f99ff70cf9645293ed
Container:
78730dcf3d0a6034a38d7e1a388b43214bd61531ae135800905610d38d9461e5
$ podman kube down --force deployment.yaml
Pods stopped:
077328593d156cf2dcdde58c9282811488ae82da732812f99ff70cf9645293ed
Pods removed:
077328593d156cf2dcdde58c9282811488ae82da732812f99ff70cf9645293ed
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
$ podman kube down --force pod.yaml
Pods stopped:
a915b73921d546fccf214557a55e9fa3c4e36b772d91975a274d30fc0c3c4344
Pods removed:
a915b73921d546fccf214557a55e9fa3c4e36b772d91975a274d30fc0c3c4344
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
test-volume
```

If you delete the pod before deleting the deployment, then you get the following:
```
$ podman kube play --start=false pod.yaml
Pod:
6c01de9c3e672e168994d08bde76ca9cf6298d6af4f8402e499d20595cd7023f
Container:
495b24e1c82c39a8b729022b80ea368ef73de41963645951563d85af42e259b2
$ podman kube play --start=false deployment.yaml
Pod:
618b0c966c01d1affff01cf884a3cbb76c87a9e369236c553fd032bbf162d2e5
Container:
250ed31482b2ecd839b3b389df925b37c1be978e412e060bd819684426fa0882
$ podman kube down --force pod.yaml
Pods stopped:
6c01de9c3e672e168994d08bde76ca9cf6298d6af4f8402e499d20595cd7023f
Pods removed:
6c01de9c3e672e168994d08bde76ca9cf6298d6af4f8402e499d20595cd7023f
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
Error: volume test-volume is being used by the following container(s): 250ed31482b2ecd839b3b389df925b37c1be978e412e060bd819684426fa0882: volume is being used
```

Furthermore, if I define the PVC in the pod yaml like so:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: test-volume
spec:
  accessModes:
    - "ReadWriteOnce"
  storageClassName: ""
---
```
I get the following error on `podman kube down pod.yaml --force`:
```
Pods stopped:
5ed0922401bc02cfdcf49e6622f60741b810b4f7303976412dcf48f759a206c3
Pods removed:
5ed0922401bc02cfdcf49e6622f60741b810b4f7303976412dcf48f759a206c3
Secrets removed:
Volumes removed:
test-volume
Error: no volume with name test-volume found in database: no such volume
```

### Describe the results you expected

The expected result would be that --force does not delete existing volumes that are not defined as PersistentVolumeClaim in the pod yaml. This could delete resources that are shared between pods outside the current pod.

```yaml

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

`--start=false` is only for ease of testing. The results are the same with or without it. I do not have access to a server with latest version as of writing this (4.8.0).

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
